project_title: Discovery Engine - eight-stage cross-disciplinary research-question discovery apparatus
runtime_target: local-first web or desktop application with a graph-backed research-stage dependency model
operational_end_state: A researcher can start with a seed question, discover transferable mechanisms from other fields, pressure-test analogies, move the result through academic research stages, and see downstream sections marked stale when upstream claims, methods, or evidence change.
core_modules: Observatory intake and problem decomposition | Scanner and source-field discovery | Bridge mechanism-transfer mapper | Cross-Pollination and XPol fit-pressure tester | Incubator research-object builder | Forge writing/export gate | stage-dependency stale-state engine
source_strategy: Use sources to strengthen cross-field mechanism transfer and academic-stage dependency. Sources should not merely answer a query; they should help move a mechanism from one field into another while marking downstream stages stale when upstream assumptions change.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output | dependency test: upstream change marks downstream work stale
current_build_status: file-backed proof build; needs live reviewer capture for stronger public demonstration
proof_to_full_build_gap: Convert file-backed proof into live reviewer capture and repeatable demo.
first_full_build: Implement a live seed-question walkthrough with one cross-field transfer and one downstream stale-state propagation.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.

final_object_type: local research operating system / workstation builder
runtime_target: desktop or local web application with room registry, instruments, state database, agents, handoffs, and export gates
operational_end_state: A researcher can create project-specific rooms, use instruments, record decisions, route work between departments or agents, preserve state, and export professional work products under review gates.
core_modules: room registry | instrument registry | state database | agent or department handoff bus | review gate | export boundary | project workstation template system
source_strategy: Use sources to make the operating environment professionally legible: rooms, instruments, records, agents, review gates, and export limits should map to recognized research, organizational, or human-computer workflow principles.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Show one project moving through room selection, instrument execution, state update, department handoff, review gate, and export.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.

product_name: Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System
final_object_type: local scholarly retrieval and evidence-warrant software
runtime_target: local corpus service with index, retrieval trace, scoring, negative-space, abstention, and citation/export layer
operational_end_state: A researcher can ask a question against a local scholarly corpus, inspect retrieved passages, see what the corpus does not support, receive an evidence score, and export only claims that remain inside the source warrant.
core_modules: corpus registry | index and retrieval service | passage trace viewer | evidence scoring | negative-space detector | abstention policy | citation/export boundary
source_strategy: Use sources to test retrieval as scholarly warrant rather than as fluent search. The proof must show corpus boundary, retrieved passage, evidence score, negative-space finding, abstention case, and claim boundary.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Build a public-safe retrieval trace with corpus boundary, retrieved passages, evidence score, negative-space finding, abstention, and claim ceiling.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.

product_name: Retrieval Integrity And Poison-RAG Evaluation Harness
runtime_target: scenario replay harness with clean/poisoned fixtures, retrieval trace, answer deformation, reviewer burden, and defense gates
operational_end_state: A researcher can run clean baseline, slow poison, fast injection, and authority-wrapper attacks through a retrieval pipeline and inspect how contamination enters, moves, distorts, or is blocked.
core_modules: adversarial fixture registry | source-admission gate | indexing and retrieval trace | answer deformation comparator | reviewer-burden estimator | block/narrow/abstain decision gate | replay ledger
source_strategy: Use sources to define retrieval threat models, poisoning pathways, evidence-chain failure points, and evaluation gates. The proof must show trace movement from source admission through retrieval, answer formation, human review, and release decision.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces
current_build_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
proof_to_full_build_gap: Build the first runnable or inspectable demo rather than leaving the project at protocol level.
first_full_build: Build the mixed-speed Poison-RAG replay with slow poison, fast injection, authority wrapper, retrieval trace, and defense decision.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo

project_title: Analyst Workstation - eight-page interactive AI-augmented intelligence-analysis handbook
runtime_target: interactive analysis application with SAT registry, baseline-vs-reviewed output, confidence delta, and release decision

project_title: Structured Methods Library - method-composition page across Heuer, structured analytic techniques, and intelligence methodology
runtime_target: method studio for composing SATs, testing fit, comparing outputs, and recording human-AI sequencing effects
operational_end_state: A researcher can select or combine structured analytic techniques, apply them to a task, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, and preserve the conditions under which each technique helped or failed.
core_modules: SAT taxonomy | method-composition builder | task-method fit checker | baseline/method comparison | sequencing experiment ledger | human-AI collaboration result board
source_strategy: Use sources to test whether each instrument actually improves analytic judgment: hypothesis discipline, bias control, decomposition, evidence weighting, confidence change, and human-AI division of labor.

project_title: Book Trilogy - Security, Dialectic, Repair : a three-book governed arc on dangerous power, democratic formation, and political repair after harm (the full manuscript corpus across 72 chapters)
runtime_target: chapter dossier system with argument, source, title, voice, visual, and handoff gates
operational_end_state: Each of 72 chapters carries a public title, argument backbone, source burden, book-specific voice rule, visual identity, revision status, and handoff to adjacent chapters.
core_modules: chapter dossier registry | argument backbone field | source state and close-read queue | voice/register constitution | visual-title governance | cross-chapter handoff | revision status
source_strategy: Use sources to govern chapter argument, voice register, public title, source burden, and cross-chapter handoff. Each source should either thicken the chapter claim, sharpen a counterclaim, or constrain the public formulation.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.

project_title: Algorithmic Active Measures - data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis
product_name: Algorithmic Active Measures - data poisoning as counterintelligence tradecraft in AI-mediated intelligence analysis
final_object_type: analytic method or human-AI evaluation module
runtime_target: structured public page plus project-specific data packet and proof runner
operational_end_state: A user can apply a method, compare baseline and method-supported reasoning, record human judgment, and preserve the claim boundary.
core_modules: baseline answer | structured review | human delta | confidence gate
source_strategy: Use sources to connect counterintelligence tradecraft, active measures, data poisoning, analytic deception, and human-AI review burden. The proof must show actor objective, poisoned evidence pathway, analytic uptake or rejection, and bounded warning.
source_requirement: Title-level routing can strengthen project planning and proof design. Public claims require project evidence or passage-level source confirmation.
source_gap: passage-level close reading still required before quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim
